cited_evidence: 2.1s VCC = 4.55 V (further drop, 0.5 V fluctuation from steady 5.05 V)
No concurrent variation in BAT.Volt (constant 22.5 V), BAT.Curr (constant 15 A), or RCOU outputs (all constant)
root_cause_analysis: DIAGNOSTIC INCONCLUSIVE: Insufficient data to determine root cause. The VCC rail dropped below the safe threshold (4.8 V) for two consecutive timestamps (2.0 s and 2.1 s) before recovering. No correlating changes in battery voltage, current, motor outputs, vibration, or IMU clipping are present in the provided data window. Without additional sensor data (e.g., BEC monitor, power module diagnostics, or longer time series), the physical cause (e.g., intermittent BEC failure, loose connector, or power module glitch) cannot be confirmed.
actionable_fix: Inspect the 5 V BEC/regulator output, check all power module connections, and review the power distribution for intermittent faults. Consider adding a dedicated VCC monitor log.
